---
title: "STA313 A2"
author: "Tala, Jinny"
date: "2026-03-06"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r, load data sets. echo = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
jobs <- read.csv('Jobs.csv')
participants <- read.csv('Participants.csv')
pubs <- read.csv ('Pubs.csv')
rest <- read.csv('Restaurants.csv')
finjour <- read.csv('Data/FinancialJournal.csv')
apartments <- read.csv('Apartments.csv')
logs <- read.csv("Data/ParticipantStatusLogs1.csv")
```

```{r, load libraries, echo = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
```

```{r -  EDA #1 }
# Exploring how often social spaces were used across the observation period: 
social_logs <- logs %>%
  filter(currentMode %in% c("AtRestaurant", "AtRecreation"))

# Count total visits per venue type
visits_summary <- social_logs %>%
  mutate(venueType = ifelse(str_detect(currentMode, "Restaurant"), "Restaurant", "Pub")) %>%
  group_by(venueType) %>%
  summarise(total_visitors = n_distinct(participantId), .groups = "drop")

# Bar chart
ggplot(visits_summary, aes(x = venueType, y = total_visitors, fill = venueType)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    title = "Total Visits to Social Venues",
    x = "Venue Type",
    y = "Number of Visits"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Pub" = "darkred", "Restaurant" = "orange")) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# kinda useless plot, results ignore temporal patterns, repeat visits, length of time spent at venue
```

```{r - EDA #2}
# Where do people spend most of their time?
mode_counts <- logs %>%
  count(currentMode) %>%
  mutate(prop = n/sum(n))

ggplot(mode_counts, aes(x=reorder(currentMode, prop), y=prop)) +
  geom_col(fill="steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title="Where Participants Spend Their Time",
    y="Proportion of Time",
    x=""
  )

# Least amount of time spent in restaurants, could it be becasue its too expensive?
```

```{r, EDA #3, warning = FALSE}
# where are social venues located across the city?

extract_xy <- function(df){
  coords <- str_extract_all(df$location, "-?\\d+\\.?\\d*")
  
  df$x <- as.numeric(sapply(coords, `[`, 1))
  df$y <- as.numeric(sapply(coords, `[`, 2))
  
  return(df)
}

pubs <- pubs %>% extract_xy()
rest <-  rest %>%extract_xy()
apartments <-  apartments %>% extract_xy()

# Mapping pub locations and their maximum occupancy 
ggplot(pubs, aes(x, y)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = maxOccupancy), color = "darkred", alpha = .7) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Pub Locations in Engagement City",
    x = "City X",
    y = "City Y"
  )

ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = pubs, aes(x, y), color = "red", alpha = .7) +
  geom_point(data = rest, aes(x, y), color = "orange", alpha = .7) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Social Venues Across Engagement City")
```
```{r, EDA #4 warning = FALSE}
# How accessible are social venues to the citizens?
# combine all venues
venues <- bind_rows(
  pubs %>% select(x, y),
  rest %>% select(x, y)
)

# compute the distance to nearest venue 
#install.packages('FNN')
library(FNN)

apartment_coords <- apartments %>% select(x, y)
venue_coords <- venues %>% select(x, y)

nn <- get.knnx(venue_coords, apartment_coords, k = 1)

apartments$distance_to_venue <- nn$nn.dist[,1]

ggplot(apartments, aes(x, y, color = distance_to_venue)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_color_viridis_c() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distance from Apartments to the Nearest Social Venue",
    color = "Distance"
  )

# Add venues in red to visualize how far apartments are from pubs and restaurants

ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = apartments,
             aes(x, y, color = distance_to_venue),
             size = 2) +
  geom_point(data = venues,
             aes(x, y),
             color = "red",
             size = 3) +
  scale_color_viridis_c() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Social Accessibility Across Engagement City"
  )
```
